message:
feat(abi): port 10 _nss_files_* tail entries (init + initgroups_dyn + 8 parse_*) (bd-2vvx3)

Close the rest of the _nss_files_* parity surface.

  - _nss_files_init() -> nss_status: returns NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS so
    both glibc-version variants (some return void, some nss_status)
    see a clean success.
  - _nss_files_initgroups_dyn(user, gid, *start, *size, **groupsp,
    limit, *errnop) -> nss_status: stub returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
    with errnop = ENOENT; output slots untouched. NSS supplementary-
    group lookup callers get the documented "no entries" path.
  - _nss_files_parse_etherent / parse_grent / parse_netent /
    parse_protoent / parse_pwent / parse_rpcent / parse_servent /
    parse_sgent: internal line parsers with signature
    (*line, *result, *parser_data, buflen, *errnop) -> int. Stub
    returns 0 ("malformed/skip") so callers walk to the next line
    without complaint. Generated via a small `nss_files_parse_stub!`
    macro.

Tests: 3 abi tests covering init success, initgroups_dyn NOTFOUND +
errnop handling (NULL and non-NULL), and parse_* zero-return for
every entry under a sample passwd-style line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vv.7","title":"Symbol expansion: signal family with safety","description":"Complete signal family coverage with async-signal-safety.

Current State:
- signal, sigaction: likely partial
- sigprocmask: likely RawSyscall
- raise, kill: likely RawSyscall
- sigsetjmp/siglongjmp: requires setjmp (bd-2ry)

Target Functions:
Basic signal handling:
- signal, sigaction
- sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask
- sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember

Signal delivery:
- raise, kill, killpg
- sigqueue (with value)

Signal waiting:
- pause, sigsuspend, sigwait, sigwaitinfo, sigtimedwait

Stack:
- sigaltstack

Non-local jump:
- sigsetjmp, siglongjmp (depends on setjmp impl)

Implementation Notes:
Signal handling is security-critical:
- Async-signal-safety requirements (can't call malloc in handler)
- Signal frame layout is architecture-specific
- SA_SIGINFO vs traditional handler signatures

TSM Considerations:
- Signal handlers run in restricted context
- Membrane must be async-signal-safe
- Validation in signal context must be fast/simple

Testing:
- Unit: signal mask operations
- Integration: signal handler registration and delivery
- Concurrency: signals + threads interaction
- E2E: Ctrl-C handling, SIGTERM graceful shutdown

Success Criteria:
- All signal.h functions classified
- Signal handlers work correctly
- No deadlocks in signal + membrane interaction

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:00.359003874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:53.952822168Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:53.952752548Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":898,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Signal family expansion complete: added 11 new ABI functions (killpg, sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask, sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember, pause, sigsuspend, sigaltstack). Version script updated with all 11 symbols. Support matrix updated (286→297 total). Reality report, README, and FEATURE_PARITY docs all updated. All quality gates pass: cargo check, clippy, fmt, signal tests (17/17), drift test.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.8","title":"Symbol expansion: process family","description":"Complete process family coverage.

Current State:
- fork, execve: likely RawSyscall
- wait, waitpid: likely RawSyscall
- getpid, getppid, getuid, etc.: RawSyscall
- system, popen: likely stubs

Target Functions:
Process creation:
- fork, vfork (deprecated but used)
- execve, execl, execle, execlp, execv, execvp, execvpe
- posix_spawn, posix_spawnp

Process waiting:
- wait, waitpid, wait3, wait4
- waitid

Process IDs:
- getpid, getppid, getpgid, getsid
- setpgid, setsid

User/Group IDs:
- getuid, geteuid, getgid, getegid
- setuid, seteuid, setreuid, setresuid
- setgid, setegid, setregid, setresgid
- getgroups, setgroups

High-level:
- system (fork + exec + wait)
- popen, pclose (fork + exec + pipe)

Implementation Notes:
- Most process syscalls are RawSyscall
- fork requires careful membrane state handling
- exec replaces address space (membrane cleanup)
- system/popen need shell invocation

TSM Considerations:
- fork: child inherits membrane state (generation counters)
- exec: membrane cleanup before exec
- Credential operations are security-sensitive

Testing:
- Unit: ID operations
- Integration: fork + exec + wait sequences
- E2E: shell command execution via system()
- Security: credential change validation

Success Criteria:
- All unistd.h process functions classified
- fork/exec sequences work correctly
- Credential operations work

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2vv.9","title":"Full symbol-universe normalization + support classification pipeline","description":"Background:
- Closing symbol gaps requires a canonical source for the full target symbol universe and support taxonomy.

Scope:
- Import and normalize full target symbol universe, then classify every symbol with current support state and confidence.
- Mark unknown/unverified symbols explicitly.

Deliverables:
1) Normalized symbol universe dataset.
2) Classification pipeline into support matrix.
3) Unknown/unverified symbol action list.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every target symbol has a non-ambiguous classification state.
- Classification output is reproducible and diff-friendly.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for symbol normalization/classification rules.
- E2E rebuild of support matrix from source data.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, family, classification, confidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.409676482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.347523879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.347441665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","support-matrix","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2vvx3","title":"PORT: 10 _nss_files_* tail entries (init + initgroups_dyn + 8 parse_*)","description":"Close the rest of the _nss_files_* parity surface — 10 entries. (1) _nss_files_init() — module initialization no-op (returns void; some glibc versions return enum nss_status, we return NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS). (2) _nss_files_initgroups_dyn(user, gid, *start, *size, **groupsp, limit, *errnop) — supplementary group lookup. Stub returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND and sets *errnop = ENOENT when non-NULL; output slots untouched. (3-10) Eight parse_* helpers — _nss_files_parse_etherent / parse_grent / parse_netent / parse_protoent / parse_pwent / parse_rpcent / parse_servent / parse_sgent. Each takes (char *line, struct *result, struct parser_data *data, size_t buflen, int *errnop) and returns int: 1 = parsed, 0 = malformed (caller skips), -1 = ERANGE. Stub returns 0 ("malformed/skip") so callers walk to the next line.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:41:54.737124230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:41:54.737124230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2wp","title":"Perf baseline suite: hyperfine + criterion for strict/hardened critical paths","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"},{"id":901,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Codex","text":"Starting execution on bd-2x5 based on bv robot triage (top actionable P0). Immediate objective: unblock allocator E2E closure by resolving the global LD_PRELOAD startup/interposition hang noted in bd-2x5.5. I will drive a reproduce -> isolate -> patch -> verify loop with deterministic logs and explicit strict+hardened evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:24Z"},{"id":902,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution update (2026-02-14): I completed a deep reproduce/isolate pass on the LD_PRELOAD allocator E2E timeout path with multiple strict/hardened trace sets. Current state: /bin/true now exits 0 in strict+hardened, allocator fixtures run and print PASS text in some runs, but processes still time out (exit 124) due recursive runtime-kernel/panic-hook interactions during interposed libc paths (observed around puts/strlen/write/posix_memalign and runtime_math observe/lock paths). I implemented guarded mitigations in runtime_policy + allocator/string guards and added runtime_policy unit tests; blocker remains unresolved for bd-2x5.5 and I attached next-step boundary in bd-2x5.5 comment.","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:22:40Z"},{"id":903,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Latest isolate update (2026-02-14): allocator fixture logic now executes to PASS output under strict+hardened, but process-termination path remains stuck (timeout 124) with repeated runtime-kernel panic fallback messages. This narrows the epic blocker to shutdown/stdio interposition recursion rather than core allocation correctness; bd-2x5.5 remains blocked pending termination-path fail-closed fix.","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:23:05Z"},{"id":904,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure evidence refresh for bd-2x5 (2026-02-19): prior LD_PRELOAD termination blocker from 2026-02-14 no longer reproduces on current head.

Validation commands and outcomes:

1) Allocator E2E fixture parity vs host (strict + hardened)
- Command: `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=20 rch exec -- bash scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh`
- Result: PASS
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc_stress host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
- Artifacts:
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/report.json`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/host/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/strict/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/hardened/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/host/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/strict/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/hardened/stdout.txt`

2) Allocator core unit-test slice (arena/quarantine/cache invariants)
- Command: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc::allocator -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
- Result: PASS
  - 23 allocator tests passed, 0 failed
  - Includes lifecycle/invariant tests and realloc/calloc/thread-cache scenarios.

This satisfies the epic’s outstanding closure blocker and provides deterministic strict+hardened evidence artifacts.

Delivered alongside design (bd-15q). EWMA-smoothed 5-signal input, 5-arm Euler-weighted scoring, O(1) integer-only. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:40:17Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-bxgwb","title":"PORT: 34 _nss_files_getXX_r NSS plugin lookup stubs","description":"Add the 34 _nss_files_getXX_r NSS plugin lookup entries to pair with the setXX/endXX stubs from bd-fsknj/bd-ukuxr. Each returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) — "I checked, no record" — and sets *errnop = ENOENT when non-NULL, so the NSS dispatch layer falls through to the next module. Signatures vary per family per the NSS files-module convention; we declare each with its canonical glibc signature so register placement of the errnop pointer matches what the caller passed. Sub-batches by arg count: 12 plain getent_r (struct, buf, buflen, errnop); 14 single-key getXby*_r (key, struct, buf, buflen, errnop); 8 multi-arg lookups (gethostbyaddr/name2/name3/name4, getservbyname/port, getnetbyaddr, gethostton/getntohost, etc).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:30:06.503436696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:30:06.503436696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-bxgwb","title":"PORT: 36 _nss_files_getXX_r NSS plugin lookup stubs","description":"Add the 36 _nss_files_getXX_r NSS plugin lookup entries to pair with the setXX/endXX stubs from bd-fsknj/bd-ukuxr. Each returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) — "I checked, no record" — and sets *errnop = ENOENT when non-NULL, so the NSS dispatch layer falls through to the next module. Host and network database entries also set *h_errnop = HOST_NOT_FOUND when that slot exists. Signatures follow /usr/include/nss.h, including getcanonname_r, gethostbyaddr2_r, gethostent_r/getnetent_r h_errno slots, getnetbyaddr_r/getnetbyname_r h_errno slots, gethostbyaddr*_r socklen_t lengths, and the ether host conversion pointer shapes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:30:06.503436696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:41:48.517137090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:37:17.428005257Z","close_reason":"shipped: 36 _nss_files_getXX_r stubs with nss.h-compatible signatures, ENOENT/HOST_NOT_FOUND slots, and focused ABI tests covering every implemented lookup shape","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-by8c","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Runtime iconv dispatch integration with explicit fallback/error policy","description":"Background:
- Runtime codec selection must remain deterministic and safe even with partial codec coverage.

Goal:
- Integrate generated codec tables into runtime iconv path with explicit fallback and error policy.

Deliverables:
1) Runtime lookup/dispatch integration.
2) Fallback behavior for unsupported codecs.
3) Mode-aware safety handling and telemetry.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Supported conversions succeed deterministically.
- Unsupported conversions fail according to explicit contract.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for supported/unsupported conversion paths.
- Structured logs with trace_id, codec_pair, dispatch_path, outcome, errno.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id":"bd-byd9","title":"EPIC: Flat Combining for Contended State [Score 6.0, section 14.2]","description":"Implement flat combining for sequentially batching operations under contention. When multiple threads contend on shared state (e.g., global allocation statistics, arena rebalancing counters, evidence ledger writes), flat combining designates one thread as the 'combiner' that executes all pending operations sequentially, eliminating lock contention. How it works: (1) Each thread publishes its pending operation to a per-thread slot in a shared publication list. (2) One thread acquires the combiner lock. (3) The combiner scans all publication slots, collects pending operations, and executes them all sequentially. (4) The combiner writes results back to each thread's slot. (5) Other threads spin-wait on their slot until the result appears. Benefits: (a) Sequential execution eliminates all synchronization within the combined batch. (b) Cache locality — the combiner's working set stays hot. (c) Throughput scales better than lock-based approaches under high contention because the combiner amortizes lock acquisition cost across N operations. Target applications in FrankenLibC: global allocation statistics (malloc_stats/mallinfo), arena rebalancing decisions, evidence ledger batch writes, and TSM validation pipeline under burst load.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern for sequential batching under contention.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R> generic implementation in src/concurrency/flat_combining.rs.
- First application: global allocation statistics (AllocStats).
- Future applications: arena rebalancing, evidence ledger batch writes, TSM pipeline burst handling.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
1. FlatCombiner generic implemented and applied to AllocStats.
2. Throughput >10x mutex at 32 threads for stat-heavy workloads.
3. No lost operations under any contention level.
4. Benchmark comparing flat combining vs mutex, RwLock, atomic counters.
5. Crossover point documented (thread count where flat combining wins).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Combiner activity at trace level. Benchmark results as JSON artifacts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:50.144746144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:41.325595364Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:41.325362824Z","close_reason":"Delivered or tracked as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1077,"issue_id":"bd-byd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:

- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-byd9.1","title":"Implement flat-combining lock for global allocation stats","description":"Implement the flat-combining primitive and apply it to global allocation statistics. Core data structure: FlatCombiner<T, Op> where T is the shared state and Op is the operation enum. Components: (1) PublicationList — array of cache-line-aligned slots, one per thread. Each slot contains: operation (Option<Op>), result (Option<Result>), active flag, age counter. (2) CombinerLock — a simple test-and-set lock. Only the combiner holds it. (3) combine() method — acquires lock, scans publication list, executes all pending ops on shared state T, writes results back, releases lock. (4) apply(op: Op) -> Result — publish op to thread's slot, try to become combiner. If successful, run combine(). If not, spin-wait on slot until result appears. First application: global allocation statistics (total_allocated, total_freed, active_allocations, peak_usage, per-size-class counts). Currently protected by a Mutex<AllocStats>. Under 32-thread workload, this mutex is a top-5 contention point. Flat combining should reduce contention by 10-50x for stat-heavy workloads (e.g., mallinfo called frequently).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler, & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern from Herlihy (2008).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R>: generic over shared state T, operation enum Op, and result type R.
- PublicationSlot: #[repr(align(128))] to span 2 cache lines (avoid false sharing on adjacent slot writes).
- Slot layout: op: AtomicPtr<Op>, result: UnsafeCell<MaybeUninit<R>>, active: AtomicBool, age: AtomicU32.
- CombinerLock: AtomicBool with try_lock() returning CombinerGuard.
- combine() scans slots, collects ops into local Vec (bounded by MAX_THREADS), executes sequentially on T, writes results back.
- apply() publishes op, tries to become combiner. If not combiner, spin_loop() on result slot with bounded spins before park().
- Thread slot assignment: thread_local! with lazy init, index from AtomicUsize::fetch_add.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. FlatCombiner<AllocStats, StatOp, StatResult> correctly computes allocation statistics under 16-thread concurrent access.
2. No lost operations: all published ops eventually executed (test: 1M ops across 16 threads, verify final stats sum).
3. Throughput >10x mutex baseline at 32 threads for write-heavy (80% write) workload.
4. Single-thread overhead <50ns per operation (combiner scan overhead amortized).
5. Fairness: per-thread throughput variance <20% (no thread starvation).
6. Publication slot memory layout verified: no false sharing (perf c2c test).
7. Works correctly when thread count exceeds slot count (graceful fallback).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: flat_combining, operation, thread_id, role=combiner|waiter, batch_size) per combine round.
- tracing::debug!(target: flat_combining, combiner_scan_time_ns, batch_size, slot_utilization) per combine.
- Periodic summary every 10K ops: total_combined, avg_batch_size, combiner_elections, wait_time_p99.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:59.941234911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:56:54.193783279Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T20:56:54.193760937Z","close_reason":"Implemented flat-combining stats + validated with deterministic and concurrent tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-byd9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-byd9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1258,"issue_id":"bd-byd9.1","author":"TopazElk","text":"Implemented flat-combining global allocation stats in malloc ABI path. Added FlatCombiningStats + publication slots + combiner lock and wired accounting in malloc/calloc/realloc/posix_memalign/memalign/aligned_alloc/free. Replaced collect_alloc_stats() approximation with live snapshot from combiner state. Test updates: malloc_abi_test now serializes tests via OnceLock<Mutex<()>> and asserts mallinfo2 tracks live bytes/active allocation count for a 1MiB allocation. Verification (offloaded via rch): cargo fmt --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test malloc_abi_test -- --test-threads=1 (15 passed). Remaining acceptance criteria (contention throughput/fairness/perf-c2c evidence) still pending; keeping bead in progress.","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:46:22Z"},{"id":1259,"issue_id":"bd-byd9.1","author":"TopazElk","text":"Added additional adversarial validation:  in  (16 threads x 4096 alloc/free ops; verifies  and  return to baseline after balanced concurrent traffic).  {"id":"bd-fnm-epic","title":"PORT: fnmatch.h native Rust impl in frankenlibc-core","description":"fnmatch lives entirely in frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs (~280 lines: classify_bracket + fnmatch_impl + pub fnmatch wrapper) with all pattern-matching logic inlined alongside the C ABI surface. -{"id":"bd-fsknj","title":"PORT: 12 _nss_files_endXX NSS plugin stubs","description":"Add the 12 _nss_files_endXX NSS plugin entries from libc.so.6. These are GLIBC_PRIVATE-versioned NSS plugin "end iteration" callbacks for the nss_files module (the loader that backs /etc/passwd, /etc/group, /etc/hosts, etc. when nsswitch.conf chooses files). Each takes no args and returns enum nss_status. NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS = 1 — "I successfully ended the iteration" — is the safe stub since we never started one. Functions: _nss_files_endaliasent / _endetherent / _endgrent / _endhostent / _endnetent / _endnetgrent / _endprotoent / _endpwent / _endrpcent / _endservent / _endsgent / _endspent.  {"id":"bd-ftchp","title":"PORT: unvis (NetBSD vis(3) streaming byte decoder)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) streaming decoder unvis(*cp, c, *astate, flag) — single-byte state-machine inverse of vis(3). Caller feeds bytes one at a time; the function returns UNVIS_VALID (1) when *cp holds a fully decoded byte, UNVIS_VALIDPUSH (2) when *cp is decoded AND the current input byte should be re-fed, UNVIS_NOCHAR (3) for partial sequences, UNVIS_SYNBAD (-1) for malformed input. The opaque *astate carries decoder state across calls. Used by mtree, BSD pkg tools, and anything needing a streaming decoder rather than the all-at-once strunvis. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::UnvisDecoder state machine + thin abi shim that maps the i32 state cell to/from the typed enum.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:10:29.760546150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:49:14.754888590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:49:14.752855201Z","close_reason":"shipped: unvis(3) streaming byte decoder + UnvisDecoder save/restore + 16 abi tests + 3 core tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-ujxkk","title":"PORT: __sigprocmask + __sigwait + __sigwaitinfo + __sigqueue + __pause + __raise + __kill + __killpg + __sigignore + __sighold + __sigrelse (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the signal-management entry points: __sigprocmask, __sigwait, __sigwaitinfo, __sigqueue, __pause, __raise, __kill, __killpg, __sigignore, __sighold, __sigrelse. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ cancellation cleanup, libpthread internals, and NPTL interruption paths link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in signal_abi (most) and unistd_abi (sigqueue/sigwaitinfo).  {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}

Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.600277266Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.734480678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.732334928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-usntv","title":"PORT: err_set_exit (BSD libutil pre-exit hook)","description":"Port the BSD libutil `err_set_exit(void (*func)(int))` setter that registers a callback invoked by err()/errx()/errc()/verr()/verrx()/verrc() immediately before they call exit(). The hook receives the exit code that err is about to use. Used by daemons/utilities to release locks, flush logs, or close PID files when a fatal error path is taken. Implementation: add a Mutex-protected global cell holding Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(c_int)> in err_abi; expose a setter that swaps and returns the previous value; modify each of the 6 fatal exit sites (err, verr, errx, verrx, errc, verrc) to invoke the hook before sys_exit_group. Tests cover the cell-management contract (set/get/swap), invocation order via a fork-based test that sets a hook writing to a shared pipe before calling errx (parent reads the pipe to confirm hook fired before exit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:33.527097940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:06.311110074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:06.309078764Z","close_reason":"shipped: err_set_exit pre-exit hook + threading through all 6 fatal exit paths (err/verr/errx/verrx/errc/verrc) + 2 abi tests covering swap-semantics and fork-based hook-fires-before-exit, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
